passages.title: Intelligent behaviour in birds
passages.source_html:
Intelligent behaviour in birds


Many people are aware of the intelligence of chimpanzees and other mammals. However, birds also demonstrate intelligent behaviour


A


For centuries, many scholars maintained that humans were the only intelligent organism on Earth. Many traits were considered to be exclusively human examples of acumen – for example, language, tool use, deception, awareness of self and others. However, exciting new research on a number of animals, particularly birds, has called into question the uniqueness of these traits, forcing us to reconsider this opinion. In 1964, people were amazed when naturalist Jane Goodalltzx first discovered chimpanzees making and using tools. But ornithologists, people who study birds. were not overly surprised. Almost 20 years earlier, a renowned ornithologist had shown that tool use was commonplace in populations of woodpecker finches residing on the Galápagos Islands. These tiny birds routinely used twigs to extract grubs from under bark.


B


Since then, the catalogue of tool-using animals has grown. At least three Australian bird species make tools similar to those of the woodpecker finch, and when white-winged choughs come across shellfish they have been known to use rocks as hammers to crack open the recalcitrant shells. Other birds show a more sophisticated level of insight. For example, black kites have been reported dropping bait into lakes to bring fish to the surface of the water, thereby making them easier to catch. A kite may also pick up a smouldering stick from an area recently burned by a bushfire and drop the stick on a patch of unburned grass. The bird then feasts on the small animals that flee from the subsequent fire.


C


Most tool-using behaviours are a means of extracting food, which may provide a clue as to how the mental abilities needed for tool use evolved. The predominant explanation is based on the proverb that 'necessity is the mother of invention'. Essentially, brain tissue is energetically expensive, so animals should have evolved only the necessary intellectual capabilities required to overcome the challenges they face in their environment. Consider a hypothetical duck grazing on a seemingly endless supply of grass. Being particularly intelligent will not help the duck eat more grass. In contrast, other species, such as birds of prey, live in a more challenging environment, where food may be distributed erratically, hidden from view or highly mobile. The food itself may be quite intelligent. So, if there are not enough resources to feed all individuals, then only the smartest in each generation will live and reproduce.


D


New Caledonian crows boast many different tools in their tool kit. They use a hooked tool made by removing all but one of the side branches from a twig. They fashion serrated rakes (using their beaks as scissors) from stiff, leathery pandanus leaves. They also make probes by modifying their own moulted feathers. Each tool is used in slightly different ways to pull grubs from deep within tree trunks. The crows carry their favourite tool from one foraging site to the next. They also store their tools for later re-use in a secure place on their perch. Problem-solving abilities have traditionally been thought to be beyond the reach of animals. Nevertheless, birds are coming up with innovative solutions all the time. Recently, New Caledonian crows were observed moulding a piece of wire, something they had never seen before, into a hook and then using it to retrieve food.


E


Literally hundreds of such reports have accumulated in back copies of scientific journals. Recently, a team of biologists from McGill University in Canada collated them and compared the frequency and size of innovations with the size of the birds' forebrain (the brain area responsible for higher-order information processing) relative to the hindbrain. The team uncovered a clear relationship: birds with relatively large forebrains are able to invent fresh solutions to ecological challenges, and to exploit the discoveries and inventions of others, more often than birds with relatively small forebrains.


F


Intelligence in birds may also arise as a result of selection to overcome the dynamic challenges of communal living. Since this involves competition between group members, to be successful, a social animal may need to be able to reflect on its own intentions, as well as those of others. The consequence of being part of a community may be the evolution of a distinctly 'political' brain.


G


What better way to exercise a political brain than to be deceitful! Perhaps the best example of deception among birds comes from the white-winged choughs. Choughs are cooperative breeders – that is, they form a communal group consisting of one breeding-pair and up to 15 non-breeding 'helpers'. However, because young choughs have so little enthusiasm for foraging, or gathering food, they are often too hungry to help. And because it is socially unacceptable to be part of a group and provide little help, young choughs often act deceptively. For example, when an adult is watching, a young chough will place some food in the mouth of a hungry chick – but it does not release the food. Instead, it waits until the adult departs and then eats it. A chough can also help the group by preening* the chicks. Interestingly, it is more likely to preen the chicks if another bird can see it do so. A chough that has been sitting totally still on the nest while the rest of the group is foraging out of sight will comically spring up and frantically start to preen the chicks as soon as some of its group members come into view. It is likely that these young choughs are only motivated to help when others are watching because they are concerned about their social status. Choughs need other choughs to like them, as they cannot breed without them.


* preening: cleaning and arranging feathers on birds

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14 - 20


The text has seven paragraphs. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph and move it into the gap.

Options (Word Bank):
A. The theory linking capacity for tool use in birds and survival
B. The influence of humans on tool use
C. The theory linking cognitive ability and living in a society
D. Reviewing long-held beliefs
E. Intelligence helps birds to remember
F. How some birds trick each other
G. Physiological evidence of birds' intelligence
H. Several examples of birds who use tools
I. One species' multiple tool-using techniques
14
正确答案： 14 D. Reviewing long-held beliefs  |  15 H. Several examples of birds who use tools  |  16 A. The theory linking capacity for tool use in birds and survival  |  17 I. One species' multiple tool-using techniques  |  18 G. Physiological evidence of birds' intelligence  |  19 C. The theory linking cognitive ability and living in a society  |  20 F. How some birds trick each other
Question 14

对应原文： A段，For centuries, many scholars maintained that humans were the only intelligent organism on Earth. Many traits were considered to be exclusively human examples of acumen-for example, language, tool use, deception, awareness of self and others. However, exciting new research on a number of animals, particularly birds, has called into question the uniqueness of these traits, forcing us to reconsider this opinion. 


详细解析：A段指出传统观点认为只有人类具有智慧，例如语言、工具使用、欺骗和自我意识等，但新的研究发现这些特征在鸟类等动物中也存在。这个发现让我们开始重新审视这些传统观念。A段的主旨句是 "However, exciting new research on a number of animals, particularly birds, has called into question the uniqueness of these traits, forcing us to reconsider this opinion." 


同义替换：reviewing= reconsider；long-held= maintained for centuries

Question 15

对应原文： B段，Since then, the catalogue of tool-using animals has grown. At least three Australian bird species make tools similar to those of the woodpecker finch, and when white-winged choughs come across shellfish they have been known to use rocks as hammers to crack open the recalcitrant shells. Other birds show a more sophisticated level of insight. For example, black kites have been reported dropping bait into lakes to bring fish to the surface of the water, thereby making them easier to catch. 


详细解析：  B段指出多种鸟类的工具使用例子，包括澳大利亚的白翅噪鸦和黑鸢等。这些鸟类的行为展示了鸟类在觅食过程中使用工具的不同方法。B段的主旨句是 "Since then, the catalogue of tool-using animals has grown." 


同义替换：birds who use tools = tool-using animals； several = catalogue of 

Question 16

对应原文： C段，Most tool-using behaviours are a means of extracting food, which may provide a clue as to how the mental abilities needed for tool use evolved.

Essentially, brain tissue is energetically expensive, so animals should have evolved only the necessary intellectual capabilities required to overcome the challenges they face in their environment.


详细解析： C段指出了鸟类的工具使用行为如何随着环境需求而进化，这种智力能力的进化是为了帮助它们在食物获取方面克服生存挑战。C段的主旨句是 "Most tool-using behaviours are a means of extracting food, which may provide a clue as to how the mental abilities needed for tool use evolved."


同义替换：capacity= mental abilities；survival = overcoming environmental challenges

Question 17

对应原文： D段，New Caledonian crows boast many different tools in their tool kit. 

Each tool is used in slightly different ways to pull grubs from deep within tree trunks.


详细解析： D段指出了新喀里多尼亚乌鸦能够使用多种工具，包括钩子、耙子等，并且在觅食过程中根据不同情况使用不同的工具。这表明它们在使用工具方面具有多样化的技巧。D段的主旨句是 "New Caledonian crows boast many different tools in their tool kit." 

Question 18

对应原文： E段，The team uncovered a clear relationship: birds with relatively large forebrains are able to invent fresh solutions to ecological challenges, and to exploit the discoveries and inventions of others, more often than birds with relatively small forebrains.


详细解析：E段指出鸟类的前脑较大与解决生态挑战的创新能力之间的关系。这是鸟类智力的生理学证据。E段的主旨句是 "The team uncovered a clear relationship: birds with relatively large forebrains are able to invent fresh solutions to ecological challenges, and to exploit the discoveries and inventions of others, more often than birds with relatively small forebrains." 


同义替换：large forebrains=physiological evidence

Question 19

对应原文： F段，Intelligence in birds may also arise as a result of selection to overcome the dynamic challenges of communal living. Since this involves competition between group members, to be successful, a social animal may need to be able to reflect on its own intentions, as well as those of others. 


详细解析：F段指出群居生活带来的挑战，认为这种环境可能导致鸟类智力的进化，特别是需要理解自己和他人的意图。 F段的主旨句是 "Intelligence in birds may also arise as a result of selection to overcome the dynamic challenges of communal living." 


同义替换：communal living=living in a society; cognitive ability=reflect on intentions

Question 20

对应原文： G段，Perhaps the best example of deception among birds comes from the white-winged choughs.

However, because young choughs have so little enthusiasm for foraging, or gathering food, they are often too hungry to help. And because it is socially unacceptable to be part of a group and provide little help, young choughs often act deceptively.


详细解析： G段指出了white-winged choughs的幼鸟如何欺骗成鸟，以此展示鸟类之间的欺骗行为。G段的主旨句是 "Perhaps the best example of deception among birds comes from the white-winged choughs."


同义替换：trick=deception

Questions 21 - 26


Choose the correct bird, (A - C), for each characteristic. You may choose any correct bird more than once.


List of Birds

A white-winged choughs

B black kites

C New Caledonian crows

Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
21

21 keeping tools that they like to use
✅ 正确答案：C

定位信息： New Caledonian crows


对应原文： D段，The crows carry their favourite tool from one foraging site to the next. They also store their tools for later re-use in a secure place on their perch.


解题思路： D段指出New Caledonian crows 会随身携带自己喜欢的工具，并将其存储在安全的地方以便以后使用。这表明它们具有保留工具的行为。
22

22 drawing out their prey by frightening it
✅ 正确答案：B

定位信息： black kites


对应原文： B段，For example, black kites have been reported dropping bait into lakes to bring fish to the surface of the water, thereby making them easier to catch. A kite may also pick up a smouldering stick from an area recently burned by a bushfire and drop the stick on a patch of unburned grass. The bird then feasts on the small animals that flee from the subsequent fire.


解题思路： B段指出了black kites 利用火将小动物吓跑后捕食这些逃跑的动物。这展示了黑鸢通过惊吓猎物将其引出从而捕食的行为。
23

23 the use of tools to remove the outer covering from food
✅ 正确答案：A

定位信息： white-winged choughs


对应原文：B段，At least three Australian bird species make tools similar to those of the woodpecker finch, and when white-winged choughs come across shellfish they have been known to use rocks as hammers to crack open the recalcitrant shells.


解题思路： B段指出 white-winged choughs 使用石块作为工具来敲碎贝壳的外壳，以便取出里面的食物。此行为符合使用工具来去除食物外壳的描述。
24

24 using food to attract their prey
✅ 正确答案：B

定位信息： using food,attract prey


对应原文：B段，For example, black kites have been reported dropping bait into lakes to bring fish to the surface of the water.


解题思路： B段指出 black kites 通过将诱饵投入湖中来吸引鱼到水面，从而更容易捕食。这符合“使用食物来吸引猎物”的描述。
25

25 the use of unfamiliar materials to make tools
✅ 正确答案：C

定位信息： unfamiliar materials,make tools


对应原文：D段，New Caledonian crows were observed moulding a piece of wire, something they had never seen before, into a hook and then using it to retrieve food.


解题思路： D段指出了 New Caledonian crows 将一根它们从未见过的金属丝弯曲成钩子来取食。这符合使用不熟悉的材料来制作工具的描述。
26

26 engaging in certain activities for the benefit of observers
✅ 正确答案：A

定位信息： engaging in activities,for the benefit of observers


对应原文： G段，Interestingly, it is more likely to preen the chicks if another bird can see it do so.


解题思路： G段指出 white-winged choughs 在有其他鸟类观察时更可能会清理雏鸟的羽毛，以此显示其社会地位。此行为表明它们在特定的活动中表现出一种“表演”性质，以获得观察者的认可。
